steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: On the left of the blank: 'Antiquarians' (noun/subject). On the right: 'to read one of Suetonius's extant works—...—might procure a copy' (infinitive phrase + main clause verb). The blank must form either a participial modifier of the noun or a coordinate verb phrase.

steps.title: Apply the participle-modifier rule
steps.content: A participle modifies a noun to form the subject of a main clause; a finite verb creates an independent clause requiring proper coordination. C) hoping correctly forms a participial phrase modifying 'Antiquarians,' allowing 'might procure' to be the main verb with proper subject connection. Options A, B, and D are finite verbs that would create two independent clauses ('Antiquarians hope/hoped/have hoped...' and '...might procure...') with no valid coordination or punctuation between them. That matches choice C — C is correct.
